name: Paradip Port
description: India's largest east-coast port and primary maritime gateway for Odisha. Handles crude oil, fertilizers, and bulk cargo. Highly exposed to Bay of Bengal cyclones.

name: Dhamra Port
description: Deep-water all-weather port on the Odisha coast operated by Adani Ports. Handles coal, bauxite, and general cargo. Strategic backup to Paradip.

name: Bhubaneswar Command Center
description: State Emergency Operations Center (SEOC) and primary command node for disaster response across Odisha. Coordinates NDRF, ODRAF, and district authorities.

name: Cuttack Logistics Hub
description: Odisha's commercial capital and central distribution node. Key junction connecting north-south and east-west logistics corridors.

name: Puri District Hub
description: Coastal district hub and tourism centre. Highly vulnerable to cyclone storm surge and flooding. Direct landfall risk from Bay of Bengal systems.

name: Balasore District Hub
description: Northern Odisha district hub bordering West Bengal. Critical node for north-corridor logistics. Frequently affected by cyclonic storms crossing from the Bay.

name: Kendrapara District Hub
description: Low-lying coastal district with high cyclone and flood vulnerability. Bhitarkanika National Park region. Key last-mile distribution point for relief supplies.

name: Jagatsinghpur District Hub
description: District surrounding Paradip Port. High industrial and port-logistics significance. Storm surge and cyclone risk directly threatens port operations and supply chains.

name: AIIMS Bhubaneswar
description: All India Institute of Medical Sciences, Bhubaneswar. Primary trauma and critical-care referral facility for eastern India. Must remain operational during all disaster scenarios.

name: SCB Medical College & Hospital
description: Shri Ram Chandra Bhanj Medical College — Odisha's largest and oldest government hospital. Critical referral centre for cyclone casualties. Over 1800 beds capacity.

name: Bhubaneswar Relief Warehouse
description: State strategic relief warehouse pre-positioned near Bhubaneswar airport for rapid aerial deployment. Stocks food kits, medical supplies, and emergency equipment.

name: Cuttack Relief Warehouse
description: Central distribution warehouse for northern Odisha districts. Positioned to serve Kendrapara, Balasore, and Bhadrak during flood or cyclone events.

name: Paradip Fuel Depot
description: IOCL fuel depot adjacent to Paradip Port. Supplies diesel, petrol, and aviation fuel for emergency vehicles, generators, and military aircraft. Extremely high risk during cyclone landfall.

name: Cuttack Railway Junction
description: Major railway junction on the East Coast Railway connecting Bhubaneswar, Balasore, and Berhampur. Critical freight and passenger hub for relief movement by rail.

name: NDRF / ODRAF Response Base
description: Combined base for National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) 2nd Battalion and Odisha Disaster Rapid Action Force (ODRAF). Rapid deployment teams with boats, vehicles, and rescue gear.

name: Coastal Cyclone Shelter Cluster
description: Network of 879 multipurpose cyclone shelters along Odisha's 480 km coastline. Built post-1999 super-cyclone to protect coastal populations. Managed by OSDMA.

name: Bhubaneswar Airport (VOBB)
description: Biju Patnaik International Airport. Critical for aerial evacuation of casualties and delivery of emergency supplies when road routes are blocked.

name: Ganjam Relief Hub
description: Southern Odisha district hub covering the Berhampur region. Significant cyclone exposure on the south-western coast. Key node for southern corridor relief operations.
